note: All 4 strategies run and log every tick. Set STRATEGY= in .env to choose which one executes orders.

vwap_scalp.name: VWAP + RSI(3) + EMA(8) Scalping
vwap_scalp.style: Scalping — very selective, few signals, high quality
vwap_scalp.description: Three indicators, each doing a different job. EMA(8) for trend direction, VWAP for session bias, RSI(3) for entry timing. All three must align before a trade fires.

indicators.ema_8: Trend direction. Price above EMA(8) = bullish momentum. Below = bearish.
indicators.vwap: Session bias. Price above VWAP = buyers in control. Below = sellers. Resets at midnight UTC.
indicators.rsi_3: Entry timing. RSI on 3 candles only — extremely sensitive. Below 30 in an uptrend = snap-back setup. Above 70 in a downtrend = reversal likely.

exit: RSI(3) crosses back above 50 (for longs) or below 50 (for shorts)
Take profit at next VWAP touch or EMA(8) cross

ema_cross.name: EMA(9/21) Crossover
ema_cross.style: Trend following — fires once per trend change, rides the move
ema_cross.description: When the fast EMA(9) crosses the slow EMA(21), momentum has shifted. Buy the golden cross (9 above 21), sell the death cross (9 below 21). RSI(14) prevents entering at extremes. Only fires on a fresh cross — not on an already-running trend.

indicators.ema_9: Fast average — reacts quickly to price changes.
indicators.ema_21: Slow average — represents the medium-term trend.
indicators.rsi_14: Confirms momentum. Keeps you out when price is already overextended.

bb_rsi.name: Bollinger Bands + RSI(14)
bb_rsi.style: Mean reversion — bets on price snapping back to the middle
bb_rsi.description: Bollinger Bands form a dynamic channel around the 20-period average. When price touches the outer edge, it has stretched too far and tends to return to the middle. RSI(14) confirms the stretch. Works best when the market is ranging, not trending strongly.

indicators.bb_upper: Upper band = 20-period SMA + 2 standard deviations. Overbought zone.
indicators.bb_lower: Lower band = 20-period SMA - 2 standard deviations. Oversold zone.
indicators.bb_middle: 20-period SMA — the target when price snaps back.
indicators.rsi_14: Confirms the extreme. RSI < 35 at lower band = strong oversold. RSI > 65 at upper band = strong overbought.

exit: Price returns to middle band (20 SMA)
Hard stop: price breaks further past the band by 0.5%

macd.name: MACD Crossover (12/26/9)
macd.style: Momentum — catches the start of a new move
macd.description: MACD measures the difference between two EMAs (12 and 26). When this difference crosses its own 9-period average (the signal line), momentum has just shifted. A bullish cross = buy. A bearish cross = sell. Only fires on a fresh cross — the entry is the moment momentum changes, not after it's already moved.

indicators.macd_line: EMA(12) minus EMA(26). Positive = recent momentum faster than medium-term.
indicators.signal_line: 9-period EMA of the MACD line. Smooths out the signal.
indicators.histogram: MACD minus signal. Positive = bullish momentum building.

long: MACD line crosses above signal line (fresh bullish cross)
short: MACD line crosses below signal line (fresh bearish cross)

exit: MACD crosses back the other direction

risk_rules: Risk maximum 1% of portfolio per trade
Maximum trade size capped by MAX_TRADE_SIZE_USD in .env
Maximum trades per day capped by MAX_TRADES_PER_DAY in .env — per strategy
Paper trade first — validate on your specific asset before going live
Run all 4 strategies for at least 2 weeks before comparing results
